title: lappa communis subsp. nemorosa
intro: Lappa communis subsp. nemorosa, also known as wood betony, is a subspecies of the Lappa communis plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, wood betony is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other conditions. The health benefits of wood betony include reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate pain and inflammation. Wood betony can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
